mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Z4 engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with the B48 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. A full engine replacement from a main dealer can cost £9,000–£13,000. Used engines can be found from £800–£2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Z4.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which engine era your Z4 has. The E89 (2009–2016) with the N20 has timing chain guide issues — the plastic guides can become brittle and deteriorate, causing excessive chain slack. Pre-2015 units are most at risk. The G29 (2018+) with the B48 is significantly more reliable — the timing chain design was improved. Common B48 issues include oil leaks from the oil filter housing, coolant leaks, and a starter motor recall on early models.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20 timing chain guide failure (E89 pre-2015) — rattling on cold start is the warning sign. B48 oil filter housing oil leaks and water pump/thermostat coolant leaks (G29). VANOS solenoid failure on both engines. Carbon buildup on intake valves due to direct injection. The B48 is a significant improvement over the N20.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and engine era. Against a well-kept G29 worth £18,000–£25,000, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 15–25% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired E89 worth £5,000–£10,000, the same recon may approach our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B48-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: E89 (2009–2016): N20B20A. G29 (2018–present): B48B20A or B48B20B. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The chain guides must be replaced — they are the weak point. A preventative replacement of the timing chain and guides costs £1,500–£3,000. A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £3,500–£5,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The B48 has far fewer timing chain issues — but regular oil changes remain essential.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential maintenance — catch it before catastrophic failure
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Straightforward gasket replacement — prevent belt damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i? Check These First
buyIf: * G29 (2018+) with the B48 engine (improved timing chain design)
* Post-2015 E89 with documented timing chain guide replacement
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
* No oil leaks around the oil filter housing (B48 — belt damage risk)
avoidIf: * Pre-2015 E89 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — the N20 is unforgiving of neglect
* Oil leaks around the oil filter housing (belt damage risk — B48)
* Overheating history or unexplained coolant loss — cooling system may be compromised
* VANOS system failure symptoms — loss of power, rough idle

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i is BMW's entry-level turbocharged petrol roadster — powered by a 2.0-litre four-cylinder engine across two generations. The E89 (2009–2016) has the N20 — with well-known timing chain guide issues, particularly pre-2015. The G29 (2018+) has the B48 — a significant improvement with the timing chain design resolved. Common B48 issues are oil leaks, coolant leaks, and a starter motor recall on early models. With typical Z4 values ranging from £8,000 (E89) to £25,000+ (G29), an engine replacement is a significant investment — the B48-engined G29 is the safer choice.

cta.label: → Compare Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z4 sDrive18i / sDrive20i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (N20 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N20 or B48) and oil-change discipline
* Timing Chain — For E89 N20: check for any chain work or cold-start rattle; pre-2015 most at risk
* Oil Leaks — Check around oil filter housing (B48 — belt damage risk)
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss may indicate water pump or thermostat issues
* VANOS Health — Test acceleration; any loss of power or sluggish response may indicate VANOS solenoid wear
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate timing, cooling or sensor problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
